arts.ttl: Not done yet: Ange, Spurs sneak into UEL semis
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou told his critics they will "have to put up with me for just a little bit longer" after Tottenham Hotspur sealed a UEFA Europa League semifinal spot with a 1-0 win against Eintracht Frankfurt in Germany.

Speaking to our Review Show afterwards, Ange said: “I’m just super proud of this group of players and the staff. A lot of teams would have been divided and given up after the season we've had, but not this group, and I'm super pleased that they've got the rewards.”

arts.ttl: You’re going to have to put up with me for a bit longer – Spurs’ Ange Postecoglou
arts.sum: Dominic Solanke’s 43rd-minute penalty earned Spurs a 2-1 aggregate win over Eintracht Frankfurt.

arts.ttl: Who will Tottenham face in Europa League semi-final if they beat Eintracht Frankfurt?
arts.sum: Ange Postecoglou is gearing up for the biggest game of his Tottenham tenure so far, as his side take on Eintracht Frankfurt in the second leg of their Europa League quarter-final on Thursday evening. Should Spurs get past Frankfurt, they will face either Norwegian side FK Bodø/Glimt or Serie A’s Lazio in the semi-finals.

arts.ttl: Tottenham Europa League semi-final opponents confirmed after dramatic penalty shoot-out
arts.sum: Tottenham Hotspur have qualified for the Europa League semi-final for the first time since 1984 after knocking Eintracht Frankfurt out in the quarter-final on Thursday night. The first leg will take place at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium on Thursday May 1 before the return leg in Italy follows a week later.

arts.ttl: Tottenham boss Ange Postecoglou sends clear message to critics after Eintracht Frankfurt win
arts.sum: EINTRACHT FRANKFURT 0-1 TOTTENHAM (1-2 AGG): Tottenham came up trumps in Germany as Dominic Solanke struck the winner from the penalty spot to book Spurs' place in the semi-finals of the Europa League

arts.ttl: Tottenham: Ange Postecoglou gets big calls right in huge Frankfurt win to extend Spurs tenure
arts.sum: Postecoglou may have rescued his Tottenham career in Frankfurt

arts.ttl: Ange in swipe at 'people who like to mock and ridicule my achievements' as Tottenham keep trophy claim alive
arts.sum: Dominic Solanke's penalty was enough for victory in Frankfurt as Spurs booked their spot in the Europa League semi-finals

arts.ttl: Solanke keeps cool from spot to send Spurs through to Europa League semi-finals
arts.athr: Ed Aarons at Deutsche Bank Park
arts.sum: Maybe Ange Postecoglou’s luck has finally turned? Having complained that the football gods were against him last week, Dominic Solanke’s penalty after an intervention from the video assistant referee – another of the Tottenham manager’s pet peeves – was enough to seal his side’s progress to the semi-finals of the Europa League. Postecoglou has probably regretted his decision to point out back in September after a defeat to Arsenal that he “always” wins trophies in his second year at a club. But after their north London rivals eased past the might of Real Madrid 24 hours earlier, Spurs also still have something to hang on to in a season that has otherwise been filled with disappointment. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Eintracht Frankfurt 0-1 Tottenham (agg 1-2): VAR to the rescue as Dominic Solanke penalty edges Spurs into Europa League semi-finals
arts.sum: MATT BARLOW AT THE DEUTSCHE BANK PARK: Ange Postecoglou's team were resilient. They were mature. They defended like lions and emerged with a clean sheet.
